questions.scamMessage: URGENT: USPS is unable to deliver your package due to incomplete address. Please verify at usps-track-pkg.top within 12hrs or it will be returned.
questions.safeMessage: Hi Mom, picked up the dry cleaning. Want me to grab milk on the way back?
questions.explanation: Real USPS will never text you a link to verify your address. The .top TLD and 'urgent' framing are classic phishing markers.

questions.scamMessage: Hi this is Officer Daniels with IRS. There is a warrant for your arrest for unpaid taxes. Call 800-555-0199 within 30 minutes or police will be dispatched.
questions.safeMessage: Your appointment with Dr. Patel is confirmed for Thursday 3:15 PM at the downtown clinic. Reply C to confirm or R to reschedule.
questions.explanation: The IRS never calls about arrests, never threatens immediate police action, and always starts contact via mail.

questions.scamMessage: Mom, my phone broke. This is my new number. I lost my wallet and need $300 to get home. Can you Zelle it now? I'll explain everything later.
questions.safeMessage: Hey, dinner Friday at 7? My place. Bringing dessert?
questions.explanation: The 'family emergency' scam — urgency, a new unknown number, and an irreversible payment method like Zelle. Always call the person directly.

questions.scamMessage: Congrats! You've been selected for a $50,000 Mercedes giveaway. To claim, send your name, address, and a $25 processing fee via Cash App to @luxgifts2026.
questions.safeMessage: Your Amazon order #112-3344556 ships today. Track at the link in your account or in the Amazon app.
questions.explanation: Legitimate prizes never require an upfront fee. Cash App tags and 'processing fees' are major scam indicators.

questions.scamMessage: Microsoft Security Alert: Your PC has been infected with 14 viruses. Call 1-855-273-1010 immediately to remove. DO NOT shut down your computer.
questions.safeMessage: Your software update finished installing. Restart when you're ready to apply the changes.
questions.explanation: Microsoft never displays support phone numbers in pop-ups. Tech-support scams use urgency + a phone number to push remote-control access.
